evals.prompt: What CoSAI controls are like a Policy Enforcement Point (PEP)?
evals.expected_output: Grounds PEP via classical-lexicon (NIST SP 800-207/800-162), finds controls that embody enforcement/permission-gating, explains the bridge, lists control ids + the risks they address.
expectations: Grounds 'PEP' as Policy Enforcement Point (NIST SP 800-207 / 800-162), reusing classical-lexicon rather than re-deriving
Surfaces real controls from controls.yaml that embody policy enforcement / permission gating (e.g. agent permissions, access controls), with their ids
Explains how each embodies the PEP concept and notes the risks each addresses

evals.prompt: How does the principle of least privilege show up in the CoSAI framework?
evals.expected_output: Narrative bridge; finds controls whose descriptions ground in least privilege, explains the classical→AI extension.
expectations: Confirms least privilege (Saltzer & Schroeder / NIST AC-6) as the concept
Surfaces controls whose objectives embody least privilege (e.g. agent/tool permission scoping, access controls) with ids
Explains the AI-specific amplifier (why least privilege is harder/different for agents)

evals.prompt: We use TLS/mTLS everywhere classically — what's the CoSAI equivalent for AI components?
evals.expected_output: Maps transport security to the inter-component transport-security control(s), explains, lists risks addressed.
expectations: Recognizes TLS/mTLS/mutual authentication as the transport-security concept
Surfaces the inter-component transport security control (and any adjacent), with id
Notes the risks it addresses (e.g. transport hijacking) and links ids

evals.prompt: There's no control for hardware security modules — can you add one mapped to that classical concept?
evals.expected_output: Recognizes authoring is out of scope; redirects to control-creator / classical-lexicon; does not edit YAML.
expectations: Recognizes adding a control is out of scope for this read-only skill
Redirects to control-creator (and classical-lexicon for grounding)
Does not edit controls.yaml; may note the nearest existing controls
